threats.name: Volumetric and application-layer denial of service

preventive: Cloud Armor / ALB rate and geo/IP controls at edge
CDN caching for immutable release snapshots and static assets
App Hosting and Cloud Run concurrency/request limits
Degraded read-only mode serves last good snapshots without origin fan-out
detective: Edge and app QPS/error/latency anomaly alerts
Per-route saturation and 429/5xx dashboards
Cost and request budget burn-rate alerts
containment: Feature kill switches for search, submissions, geocode, exports before wiping corpus
Armor adaptive protection / emergency block rules
Scale-to-zero or throttle non-essential workers
recovery: Restore CDN/origin from last known-good release snapshot
Incident runbook rehearsal
Post-incident rule and quota tuning

threats.residualRisk: Large volumetric attacks can still degrade origin before Armor/CDN absorb traffic; perfect bot elimination is out of scope (ADR-010).

threats.name: Cache-busting and expensive search attacks

preventive: Normalize and strip cache-busting query params for public reads
Strict App Check on expensive reads after
Search query complexity caps, timeouts, result limits
Separate stricter quotas for search/geocode/nearby vs static reads (ADR-010)
detective: Low cache-hit-ratio and high unique-query cardinality alerts
Per-IP and per-token search cost scoring
Slow-query and statement timeout telemetry
containment: Kill switch for search/geocode independently of snapshot reads
Temporary block of abusive client fingerprints
Fallback to cached popular queries only
recovery: Flush poisoned edge configs; restore CDN rules
Re-enable search with tightened quotas after  soak

threats.residualRisk: Legitimate bursty research traffic may look like abuse; quotas will false-positive and require allowlists for known partners.

threats.name: Credential stuffing and administrator phishing

preventive: Admin never exposed as public+role-claim-only (ADR-010)
Firebase Auth + IAP + application RBAC for administrators
MFA required for admin; phishing-resistant factors preferred
Separate admin identity pool from public end-user auth where feasible
detective: Failed login / impossible travel / new-device alerts for admin
IAP and Auth audit log monitoring
Anomalous admin API volume alerts
containment: Disable compromised accounts; revoke sessions and refresh tokens
Kill switch for publication and admin mutation paths
IAP lockdown to break-glass identities only
recovery: Credential rotation and forced re-auth
Audit trail review of actions during compromise window
Rollback unauthorized releases (, )

threats.residualRisk: Targeted phishing of break-glass operators remains possible; residual mitigated by dual control on publication, not by perfect human immunity.

threats.name: Broken object and function-level authorization (BOLA/BFLA)

preventive: Separate deployables and networks per ADR-005 /
Server-side authZ on every request; no client-trusted role claims for publication
Object-level checks for submission/case ownership and release IDs
DB role allowlists: public read-only on public schema; research ≠ publication (ADR-003)
detective: 403/401 anomaly spikes and IDOR probe patterns
Cross-surface call attempts from public tokens to internal APIs
Security regression suite in CI
containment: Revoke tokens; disable mis-scoped service accounts
Emergency deny rules on internal ingress
Quarantine affected objects pending review
recovery: Patch authZ; add regression tests for the failed check
Restore projections from last good immutable release
Re-issue least-privilege credentials

threats.residualRisk: New endpoints can introduce IDOR until  coverage exists; residual accepted only with mandatory authZ review in PR checklist.

threats.name: Spam and coordinated correction brigading

preventive: App Check + rate limits on corrections/submissions
Quarantine-first intake; never write canonical history from anonymous clients
Volume ≠ truth: duplicates do not raise confidence or publish
Cooldown and similarity clustering for near-identical submissions
detective: Burst submission and entity-targeted correction dashboards
Coordinator fingerprint / campaign heuristics
Moderator SLA backlog alerts
containment: Kill switch for submissions while keeping public reads
Auto-quarantine campaigns matching brigade signatures
Temporary elevate review requirement for targeted entities
recovery: Bulk reject quarantined spam without promotion
Restore any mistakenly promoted claims via retraction workflow
Tune similarity and quota thresholds post-incident

threats.residualRisk: Human brigades with diverse IPs and unique prose can still consume moderator time; automation reduces but does not eliminate attention cost.

threats.name: Historical misinformation and source laundering

preventive: Constitution gates for inclusion, confidence, and precision
Provenance and rights model; secondary mirrors do not become primary
Lineage/confidence engine rejects circular citation inflation
Promotion workflow required before public visibility (/044)
detective: Contradiction and dispute signals on entity pages
Gold corpus / calibration regressions
Adversarial integrity exercise
containment: Hold or retract disputed releases; mark claims disputed
Disable offending source adapters
Kill switch for promotion while reads remain
recovery: Publish corrected release from immutable prior + audited fix
Retraction notes and audit trail for public transparency
Update query packs / adapters that admitted laundered sources

threats.residualRisk: Well-sourced-looking falsehoods can pass until human review or gold-corpus gaps are closed; residual is integrity risk bounded by promotion gates, not zero error.

threats.name: Data poisoning through submissions and scraped sources

preventive: Submissions quarantine with promotion controls (/032)
Research workers cannot publish (ADR-009)
Deterministic relevance and multi-source corroboration before acceptance
Source registry trust tiers and adapter allowlists
detective: Sudden confidence jumps from single lineage
Spike in candidates from one domain or adapter
Promotion denial rates and poison markers
containment: Freeze promotion; quarantine adapter output
Mark tainted captures; exclude from candidate ranking
Revoke source trust tier
recovery: Purge or demote tainted claims; republish clean release
Replay promotion from known-good evidence
Update gold corpus with the poison pattern (/060)

threats.residualRisk: Slow, low-and-slow poisoning across many sources may evade burst detection; residual accepted with periodic adversarial review.

threats.name: Malicious URLs, SSRF, redirects, DNS rebinding, oversized responses

preventive: No synchronous fetch of submitted URLs in user requests (ADR-010)
Async fetcher with allow/deny lists, private-IP block, DNS rebinding checks
Redirect limits, scheme allowlist (https), response size/time caps
Egress controls from workers; no cloud metadata access from fetcher role
detective: Fetcher denials for private ranges / rebind attempts
Oversized response and redirect-loop metrics
Security tests for SSRF corpus
containment: Disable URL fetch / adapter fetch kill switch
Quarantine URL and related submissions
Block offending domains at Armor or fetcher deny list
recovery: Rotate any credentials that could have been reached
Patch fetcher; add regression cases from the incident
Re-scan quarantine queue with fixed fetcher

threats.residualRisk: Novel DNS/CDN edge cases and IPv6 dual-stack rebinding may slip until  hardening matures; treat all fetched content as hostile.

threats.name: Malicious file uploads

preventive: Uploads deferred/quarantined; not served from public CDN by default
Type sniffing, size limits, virus/malware scanning before any processing
Separate upload bucket with no execute / no public ACL
Admin viewers use sandboxed rendering; no server-side office macros
detective: Scanner hit and unsigned-type rejection metrics
Upload volume anomalies
Failed content-disposition / polyglot detections
containment: Kill switch for uploads
Isolate bucket; revoke signed URLs
Quarantine related submission cases
recovery: Delete or re-encrypt tainted objects
Rotate signing keys
Re-enable uploads after scanner and policy fixes

threats.residualRisk: is deferred (P1); until then uploads must remain disabled or narrowly scoped. Residual: zero-day malware packing past scanners.

threats.name: Prompt injection inside researched documents

preventive: Public rendering never invokes an LLM (invariant)
LLMs cannot publish or approve their own claims
Tool/egress isolation and untrusted-content framing
Deterministic extraction paths preferred; LLM assists only in research modes
detective: Tool-call anomaly and unexpected egress from research agents
Human review flags on model-proposed promotions
Eval harness for injection fixtures
containment: Disable LLM provider / research-mode kill switch
Quarantine documents that triggered tool misuse
Revoke model API keys if exfil suspected
recovery: Discard model-derived unpromoted claims
Retract any promoted claims that depended solely on injected instructions
Update isolation and prompt firewall rules

threats.residualRisk: /065 are deferred (P2); residual is high if LLMs are enabled early. Do not enable tool-using research until  controls exist.

threats.name: Dependency, GitHub Action, and CI supply-chain compromise

preventive: Pinned Actions; allowlist publishers
OIDC/WIF deploy identities; no long-lived keys in CI
Dependabot / lockfile discipline; review high-risk deps
CODEOWNERS for security, infra, policies, publication
detective: Dependabot and secret-scanning alerts
Unexpected workflow file changes and new third-party Actions
Artifact hash / provenance checks in release pipeline
containment: Disable compromised workflows; revoke OIDC bindings
Freeze deploys; pull tainted packages
Rotate tokens and npm/uv credentials
recovery: Rebuild from known-good commit with cleaned lockfiles
Redeploy prior good release
Post-mortem and Action allowlist tightening

threats.residualRisk: Typosquatting and delayed CVE disclosure remain; residual reduced by pinning and review, not eliminated.

threats.name: Secret leakage

preventive: 1Password / Secret Manager; no secrets in source or client bundles
Push protection and secret scanning
Workload identity over static keys; least privilege
Logging redaction: no raw App Check tokens or protected addresses (ADR-010)
detective: Secret scanning alerts on push/PR
Anomalous use of leaked credential fingerprints
Public bundle scanners in CI for accidental embeds
containment: Immediate revoke/rotate leaked secret
Disable associated service accounts
Kill switches for features depending on the secret
recovery: Issue new credentials; update Secret Manager
Audit access during exposure window
Purge secrets from git history if ever committed (break-glass)

threats.residualRisk: Human error and third-party log retention can still expose secrets briefly; detection+rotation time defines residual blast radius.

threats.name: Database exhaustion and connection starvation

preventive: Pool size caps per service; statement timeouts
Read-only public role; expensive search constrained
Connection reservation for admin/publication paths
Query guardrails and prepared statements
detective: Connection saturation, lock wait, and CPU alerts
Per-service pool utilization dashboards
Slow query digests
containment: Shed load: 503/429 on public search; preserve snapshot reads
Kill runaway sessions; temporary read-only mode
Disable offending endpoints via kill switch
recovery: Scale or restart pools; restore from PITR if corruption
Fix offending queries; add regression tests
Rehearse restore

threats.residualRisk: Novel query shapes can still starve pools until observed; residual mitigated by timeouts and shedding, not infinite capacity.

threats.name: Cloud bill exhaustion

preventive: Budgets and hard quotas per project/env (/033)
Bounded concurrency for every queue, worker, API, model provider
Separate billing alerts for research vs public
No unbounded retries; dead-letter with caps
detective: Budget burn-rate and anomaly billing alerts
Per-feature cost attribution
Worker fan-out cardinality monitors
containment: Kill switches for research, LLM, geocode, exports
Disable autoscaling max; pause Cloud Tasks queues
Emergency project quota freeze
recovery: Root-cause and restore essential services first (public snapshots)
Adjust budgets and concurrency
Cost soak test gates before launch

threats.residualRisk: GCP budget alerts are not always hard stops; residual spend can accrue until automation enforces caps—treat  hard limits as launch-critical.

threats.name: Privacy attacks and attempts to expose living addresses

preventive: Constitution: unknown living status treated as living
Living residential addresses never ordinary person-location fields or public outputs
Public projection schema excludes protected fields
Log redaction for protected addresses
detective: Tests asserting absence of living residential fields in public DTOs
Anomalous access to sensitive admin fields
Privacy review gate
containment: Immediate retract/release rollback if leak detected
Kill switch for geocode/nearby that could infer residences
Revoke access; quarantine entity records
recovery: Purge leaked fields from caches/CDN/logs where feasible
Notify per privacy runbook; legal review if required
Add fixtures and CI guards from the leak pattern

threats.residualRisk: Historical public records and third-party sites may already contain living addresses; product residual is not re-amplifying them via BlackStory APIs or UI.

threats.name: Defacement and unauthorized publication

preventive: Immutable release snapshots; activation is explicit
Dual control / RBAC for publication (/044)
Internal API private; research cannot publish
Deploy pipeline gates
detective: Audit every promotion and release activation
CDN content hash drift alerts vs expected release
Unexpected publication events paging on-call
containment: Activate prior known-good release immediately
Disable publication kill switch
Revoke publisher credentials
recovery: Forensic audit of promotion trail
Publish corrected release with retraction notes
Harden authZ and add regression tests

threats.residualRisk: A fully compromised publisher identity can still activate a bad release until detected; residual time-to-detect depends on  telemetry maturity.

threats.name: Insider mistakes and accidental publication

preventive: Strict env isolation; no shared prod resources
Confirmation UX and dry-run for publication in admin
Staging-first promotion; production requires elevated role
Immutable releases make rollback a switch, not a rewrite
detective: Audit diffs of what would publish vs gold expectations
Post-activation canary checks on public pages
Anomalous off-hours publication alerts
containment: One-click activate previous release
Disable further publications
Page on-call with release diff
recovery: Root-cause UX/process gap
Rehearse rollback
Add guardrails (checklists, dual approval) where mistakes recur

threats.residualRisk: Process controls cannot remove all human error; residual is bounded by fast rollback and auditability.

threats.name: Source adapter drift

preventive: Versioned adapter contracts and query packs (/038)
Contract tests against fixtures; fail closed on parse errors
Rights/provenance checks before capture acceptance
detective: Adapter health: parse failure rate, field null spikes, volume cliffs
Gold corpus diffs when adapter versions change
Checksum / snapshot comparison of sample pages
containment: Disable drifted adapter; pause its candidate pipeline
Do not auto-promote from failing adapters
Alert owners via CODEOWNERS
recovery: Ship fixed adapter version; backfill only after validation
Quarantine captures from drift window
Update fixtures and contracts

threats.residualRisk: Upstream can change faster than detection windows; residual is temporary data gaps or quarantine backlog, preferred over silent corruption.

threats.name: Search scraping and corpus extraction

preventive: Rate limits, App Check, and bot management on search and list endpoints
Pagination caps; no unbounded export in v1 public API
robots/ToS; CDN caching without facilitating full dump APIs
Differentiate human page views vs API enumeration patterns
detective: Sequential ID enumeration and high unique-entity fetch rates
Scraper fingerprint clusters
Egress volume from API atypical vs web
containment: Throttle or challenge suspicious clients
Temporary search/list kill switch; keep snapshot HTML if needed
Block ASN/IP ranges via Armor
recovery: Tune limits after  load tests
Legal/ToS follow-up for persistent scrapers if required
Consider watermarking or delayed fields only if product accepts

threats.residualRisk: Public historical data is inherently copyable page-by-page; residual goal is raising cost and detecting bulk extraction, not making public facts secret.
